data.title: Stride rallies as FQ1 results, guidance crushes Wall Street's expectations
data.description:
Shares of Stride (NYSE:LRN) were launched more than 22% higher in Tuesday’s after-hours after the company crushed FQ1 expectations and issued guidance that was significantly better than Wall Street’s estimates.

If extended into regular trading, the postmarket rally could potentially reverse losses from a 10-day losing streak and offset a 10% sell-off tied to a short report from Fuzzy Panda.

Stride (LRN), which provides on-line education curriculums and related educational services, earned a profit of $0.94 per share, nearly 9x more than a year ago and $0.73 better than expected. A 14.8% gain in revenue to $551.1M beat estimates by $47M.

Enrollments during the company’s fiscal first quarter increased 18.5% to 222.6K.

For FY25, Stride (LRN) is forecasting revenue to be between $2.225B and $2.30B compared to the consensus estimate of $2.17B. Adjusted operating income is targeted for $395M to $425M versus $293.9M last year.

Total sales for the fiscal second quarter are projected to be between $560M to $580M versus $535.35M estimates. Adjusted operating income is expected to be between $115M to $125M versus $95.9M in the same quarter last year.

data.title: Victory Battery Metals announces share consolidation and name change
data.description:
• Victory Battery Metals (OTCPK:VRCFF) on Tuesday said it intends to change its name to "Supreme Critical Metals Inc" and consolidate its common shares on the basis of ten pre-consolidation common shares for one post-consolidation common share of the company.
  • The company will trade on the CSE under the new symbol "CRIT".
  • The company currently has 47.4M common shares issued and outstanding. Accordingly, once the share consolidation is effective, it will have 4.74M common shares issued and outstanding.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aptar receives $4.8M U.S. government contract to advance sterilization technology
data.description:
• AptarGroup (NYSE:ATR) on Tuesday said the U.S. Federal Government has awarded the company a five-year contract worth $4.8M to advance development of its ActivShield technology.
  • The technology sterilizes medical devices and instruments without the need for a power source, making it a solution for rural areas, military settings and healthcare facilities with limited or no current sterilization capability, the company said.
  • ATR +1.24.% after hours to $167.87.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CrossFirst Bankshares Non-GAAP EPS of $0.43 beats by $0.04, revenue of $67.1M beats by $1.42M

data.title: Douglas Elliman director Liebowitz to succeed Lorber as chairman and CEO
data.description:
• Douglas Elliman (NYSE:DOUG) named a member of its board, Michael Liebowitz, to succeed Howard M. Lorber as the company's chairman and CEO.
  • Lorber, who is 78, decided to retire, the company said on Tuesday.
  • Liebowitz, who is 55, is an entrepreneur, private investor, and business executive with extensive experience founding, acquiring, and monetizing businesses in the insurance and financial industries.
  • Douglas Elliman (DOUG) stock fell 0.4% in after-hours trading.

data.title: First Quantum Minerals Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14 beats by $0.19, revenue of $1.28B beats by $180M
data.description:
• First Quantum Minerals press release (OTCPK:FQVLF): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.14 beats by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $1.28B (-36.9% Y/Y) beats by $180M.
  • Guidance: Guidance has been updated to reflect performance year-to-date and the outlook for the remainder of the year. Copper production guidance has narrowed to a range of 400,000 – 420,000 tonnes from 370,000 – 420,000 tonnes. Strong performance from Kansanshi, Guelb Moghrein and Cayeli has resulted in an increase in copper production guidance for these operations. Sentinel guidance range has been narrowed with the upper end reduced based on performance to date. Gold production guidance has increased to 120,000 – 135,000 ounces from 95,000 – 115,000 ounces to reflect higher grades experienced to date at Kansanshi. Nickel production guidance remains unchanged.
  • Copper unit cost guidance has been narrowed for both C11 and AISC1 to reflect performance to date, coupled with a favourable Zambian kwacha/US dollar exchange rate and strong by-product credits, partially offset by increased Zambian electricity costs. Guidance does not include any P&SM costs with respect to Cobre Panamá. C1 cash costs1 guidance assumes a gold price of $2,500 per ounce for the remainder of the year, an average Brent Crude oil price of $85 per barrel and a Zambian kwacha/US dollar exchange rate of 25.
